rulings.Case Title: RODERICK BROOKS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cummins Engine Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Cummins Engine Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability), 4 (loss of consortium), 5(component parts) and 7 (loss of consortium) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 6 (false representation) and issue 8 (punitive damages) because those claims have been dismissed. Mr. Brooks¿ deposition testimony and declaration create a triable whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing engine gaskets attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Brooks¿ deposition testimony are overruled. Issue 5 seeks adjudication of less than an entire claim which is prohibited by the summary adjudication statute. Because plaintiffs were married as of the date that Mr. Brooks suffered from an asbestos-related disease, defendant¿s argument on issue 7 lacks merit. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW STINSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Sii'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Pneumo Abex, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 3 (battery) and denied as to issues 1 (negligence) and 2( strict liability). Plaintiff¿s declaration and defendant¿s GO 129 interrogatory responses creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing friction products attributable to defendant. The objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW STINSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant B. Panela Drayage Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant B. Panella Drayage Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Construing all of the evidence presented by plaintiff liberally, there is insufficient evidence that any retained control by defendant affirmatively contributed to Mr. Stinson¿s exposure to asbestos. (Millard v. Bisources (2007) 156 Cal. App. 4th 1338; Michael v. Denbeste Transportation (2006) 137 Cal. App. 4th 1082; Park v. Burlington Northern (2003) 108 Cal. App. 4th 595) =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW STINSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Borg-Warner Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Borg-Warner Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable fact whether he was to asbestos-containing clutches attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of making a prima facie showing that: 1) Mr. Stinson does not have an asbestos-related disease and 2) Mr. Stinson¿s exposure to defendant¿s clutches as not a substantial factor in causing Mr. Stinson¿s asserted asbestos-related disease. The objections to Dr. Sawyer¿s declaration are sustained. The objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HENRY SCHECHINGER VS. AMERICAN STANDARD, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. DEFENDANT AUTOZONE WEST, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(220/HEK)
